title: HEALTH EQUITY - 211info
text: NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALTH EQUITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps connect the community to resources that address social factors affecting health and provide culturally relevant health education and health policy advocacy. Services include information and referral, mental health groups, talking circles, and traditional and family wellness opportunities. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identified as Native Americans Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 288-8177 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus line 75 Services: Advocacy * Health Issues * Native American Community Mental Health Related Support Groups * Native American Community Specialized Information and Referral * Native American Community Wellness Programs * Native American Community Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND FINANCE - MICRO ENTERPRISE EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EARLY HEAD START EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - MANY NATIONS ACADEMY EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - COLLEGE AND CAREER SERVICES EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMELESS YOUTH SERVICES HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - NAYA GENERATIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FOSTER CARE SUPPORT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - POSITIVE INDIAN PARENTING LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - CAMPS AND SEASONAL PROGRAMMING LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - CULTURAL ARTS LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - RECREATION MUTUAL SUPPORT - HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CHXI SAN PLAYGROUP UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
